channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Fireworks in Milan as Inter clinch Serie A title with a 2-0 win over Parma
item.description: Inter Milan win their 21st Scudetto, edging Napoli, with three matches remaining in the Italian football league season.

item.title: Detained Gaza aid flotilla activists arrive in Netherlands
item.description: Two activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla arrived in the Netherlands after being released from Israeli custody.

item.title: Trump says US to begin escorting ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to escort stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil prices flat as Trump’s plan for Strait of Hormuz fails to calm market
item.description: Brent crude is largely unmoved by Trump's announcement of the operation, dubbed Project Freedom.

item.title: Illegal ‘free party’ at French military site draws up to 40,000 ravers
item.description: Tens of thousands of partygoers gathered for an illegal “free party” at a military firing range near Bourges.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran warns Trump’s Hormuz mission will violate ceasefire
item.description: Iran protests as Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to free stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Thousands in India’s Manipur mark three years since ethnic clashes began
item.description: Thousands of protesters gathered in India’s Manipur state to mark three years since ethnic violence erupted.

item.title: Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
item.description: Three of six passengers ill from a suspected rodent-transmitted virus have died, and one is in intensive care, WHO says.

item.title: French left-wing’s Melenchon says he will run for president in 2027
item.description: Melenchon says he will make fourth bid for presidency with President Macron term-limited and Le Pen facing ban.

item.title: Real Madrid stays alive in La Liga title race with victory at Espanyol
item.description: Two goals by Vinicius Jr allowed Madrid to remain in title contention with Barcelona ahead of El Clasico next Sunday.

item.title: Iran lawmaker says Strait of Hormuz will not return to pre‑war state
item.description: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz will never return to the status quo that existed before the US-Israel war on Tehran.

item.title: Trump says US will ‘help free up’ ships stuck in Hormuz Strait
item.description: US president says any interference with operation, which will start on Monday, will 'have to be dealt with forcefully'.

item.title: Is Mali’s military government losing control?
item.description: A coalition of armed groups and rebels challenges the military government's authority.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Miami GP to extend F1 title lead
item.description: Nineteen-year-old becomes the first driver in F1 history to convert his first three pole positions into race victories.

item.title: World Press Freedom Day marked in Gaza as journalist death toll rises
item.description: Palestinian journalists in Gaza marked World Press Freedom Day by honouring colleagues killed and targeted by Israel.

item.title: Is Israel expanding into Gaza despite ceasefire?
item.description: Redi Tlhabi speaks to Ray Adams Row Farr from Forensic Architecture about Israel’s expansion into Gaza post-ceasefire.

item.title: Germany’s Merz downplays rift with Washington despite US troop drawdown
item.description: German chancellor says criticism of Donald Trump's strategy in Iran unrelated to troop withdrawal announcement.

item.title: Israel’s strategy of ‘permanent war’: A race against time?
item.description: Political analyst Daniel Levy says US policy is so ‘marinated’ in Israeli narratives, the two are indistinguishable.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian man in raid on Nablus in occupied West Ban
item.description: Israeli forces have raided the city of Nablus in the occupied West Bank, killing a 26-year-old man.

item.title: Investigators say Trump assassination suspect shot officer at press gala
item.description: Secret Service agent at White House correspondents' dinner was 'definitively' hit by suspect's bullet, prosecutor says.

item.title: Floods and landslides kill at least 18 in Kenya
item.description: Ongoing rains are causing floods and landslides in the East African nation.

item.title: Manchester United beat Liverpool to secure Champions League qualification
item.description: Kobbie Mainoo's late strike earns a thrilling victory after bitter rivals Liverpool had fought back from two goals down.

item.title: Bulk carrier attacked by multiple small craft off Iran, UKMTO says
item.description: Crew is safe and no environmental impact has been reported after the incident 11 nautical miles (20km) west of Sirik.

item.title: The US vs Latin America: Varsha Gandikota and Susana Muhamad
item.description: Gandikota asks Muhamad: What does the military‑industrial machine mean for the sovereignty of the Global South?

item.title: Israel threatens Gaza war resumption to force disarmament as ‘truce’ frays
item.description: Palestinian factions reject US-backed plans linking aid to weapons surrender, demanding clear political path forward.
